mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is ideal for applications requiring semantic search and matching capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for careful resource allocation and configuration for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Qdrant Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Qdrant Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Qdrant using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.github.io/qdrant-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Qdrant Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Qdrant CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Qdrant to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/crds/qdrant.io_qdrants.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Qdrant using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant database in the specified namespace using Helm, ensuring that the correct version is deployed.

```bash

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --create-namespace --version 1.17.0

```

> Installs Qdrant in the 'qdrant' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Qdrant Collection
steps.description:
This step creates a sample collection in Qdrant to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name": "example_collection", "vector_size": 128}'

```

> Creates a collection named 'example_collection' with a vector size of 128.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name": "example_collection", "vector_size": 128}'

steps.title: Verify Qdrant Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Qdrant pod is running and the collection is created successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections'

```

> Verifies that the Qdrant pod is in a running state.
> Lists all collections to confirm 'example_collection' exists.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Qdrant, including collections and vectors.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

kubectl delete namespace qdrant

```

> Uninstalls Qdrant from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation post-upgrade.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections'

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --version 1.17.0

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Backup your collections data.
> Upgrades Qdrant to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Qdrant pod is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant pod may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and review logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n qdrant'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Collection not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified collection does not exist in Qdrant.

**Fix:**
Ensure the collection was created successfully using the correct API endpoint.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Qdrant.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Qdrant Helm chart values or scale your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The configuration file may contain invalid parameters.

**Fix:**
Review the configuration file for any typos or unsupported options.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new vector search algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Qdrant pod running with the output of 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' showing the pod status as 'Running'. Additionally, the collection should be listed when querying collections.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name": "example_collection", "vector_size": 128}'
kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
